scenario.title: The Event Schema
scenario.context: You're building a calendar integration that needs to sync events from AI-generated meeting summaries. The downstream system expects events in a specific schema with proper date/time handling.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that produces calendar events matching a precise schema with proper datetime formatting and timezone handling.
constraints: Attendees must be an array of email strings
Required fields: title, start_time, end_time, organizer
scenario.exampleInput: Meeting summary: 'Team standup tomorrow at 9am EST for 30 minutes. Attendees: john@company.com, sarah@company.com. Organized by mike@company.com.'
hints: Be explicit about datetime format with examples
Show the exact schema with field types

rubric.name: DateTime Format
rubric.description: Is the datetime format precisely specified?

scoringGuide.excellent: ISO 8601 format shown with example, timezone handling clear

rubric.name: Schema Structure
rubric.description: Is the full schema clearly defined?

scoringGuide.excellent: All fields with types and requirements shown

rubric.name: Field Types
rubric.description: Are data types specified for each field?

scoringGuide.excellent: Types for all fields with constraints
Array structure with item type defined

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Extract calendar event details from the meeting summary and return JSON matching this exact schema:

```json
{
  "title": "string - Event title",
  "start_time": "string - ISO 8601 UTC (e.g., 2024-01-15T14:00:00Z)",
  "end_time": "string - ISO 8601 UTC",
  "duration_minutes": "integer",
  "organizer": "string - email address",
  "attendees": ["array of email strings"],
  "location": "string or null",
  "description": "string or null"
}
```

Rules:
- Convert all times to UTC (Z suffix)
- Today's date is 2024-01-15 for relative references like 'tomorrow'
- EST = UTC-5, so 9am EST = 14:00 UTC
- Calculate end_time from start_time + duration
- If location not mentioned, use null

Return ONLY valid JSON.

Meeting summary:
[text here]
commonMistakes: Not showing how to handle relative dates
